claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema correctly implements research/fetch evidence support with external/project scope separation, boundary audit enforcement, and PRD readiness blocking on violations. Real Claude agent stress testing confirms implementation matches specification.

evidence.summary: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes WebFetch and WebSearch, confirming research/fetch capability is available to agents.
evidence.why: This validates the system supports external research tools as required by the demand.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes conditional instruction: 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: This confirms the agent protocol explicitly separates external research from project facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies evidence as project/external/user/unknown based on path, url, source, and scope fields.
evidence.why: This is the core evidence scope classification logic that prevents external evidence from satisfying project facts.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence lacks project scope.
evidence.why: This enforces the requirement that existing-project facts must have project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures file state via git ls-files or filesystem walk, excluding allowed write roots, using SHA-256 digests.
evidence.why: This implements the boundary audit mechanism that detects unauthorized project mutations.

evidence.summary: Boundary violation injection: constructs BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, forces prd_ready=false, spreads into final readiness.
evidence.why: This confirms boundary violations block PRD readiness as required by the demand contract.

evidence.summary: Real Claude agent dispatch results show explorer (14 evidence items), cross-checker (17 evidence items), and verifier (6 evidence items) all completed successfully with project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: This is stress test evidence that real Claude -p agents work correctly with the dispatch system.

evidence.summary: Boundary audit result: project_mutation='clean', changes=[], confirming no target-project files were modified during agent execution.
evidence.why: This validates boundary enforcement works correctly during real agent execution.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker is generated.
evidence.why: This confirms evidence scope validation is enforced by the router logic.

evidence.summary: Tests verify boundary blocks project file mutation and git-backed detection works, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker generation.
evidence.why: This confirms boundary audit behavior is tested and working as specified.

evidence.summary: Doctrine documents boundary enforcement: 'dispatch 会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成 readiness blocker.'
evidence.why: This confirms implementation matches documented design intent.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject parses agent JSON output with repair logic for common formatting issues like line ranges and spacing.
evidence.why: This shows the dispatch handles real-world agent output robustness.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts between agent recommendations and converts them to blockers instead of averaging.
evidence.why: This confirms agent disagreement handling prevents false consensus.

risks: If gitFiles fails silently in non-git repositories, boundary detection may not fall back correctly to walkFiles.
If CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS list becomes outdated, new Claude tools may not be covered by boundary audit.
If evidence scope classification logic misses edge cases, external evidence could incorrectly satisfy project facts.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch fully implements research/fetch support with proper scope separation. CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes WebFetch/WebSearch, agent protocols explicitly mark external evidence, router scope validation prevents external-only evidence from satisfying project facts, and boundary audit enforcement blocks PRD readiness on violations. Real Claude agent stress testing (.yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-20260601010123669-6lzyiq/) confirms implementation works correctly with clean boundary audit and comprehensive project-scoped evidence from explorer/cross-checker/verifier.
